chunks.text:
Mitigating the risk of illegal content
Mitigating the risk of illegal content
Online safety
Illegal and harmful content
Research, statistics and data
Published:
17 May 2023
This qualitative research gives a snapshot of the ways in which some platforms are seeking to mitigate the risks associated with user-to-user interactions on platforms.
One report focuses on how services are seeking to mitigate the risk of user generated content fraud, while the other report considers how services are approaching mitigating a wider range of illegal harms.
Ofcom has carried out this research to help build our evidence base as we prepare to implement the new online safety laws. The findings should not be considered a reflection of any final policy position that Ofcom may adopt when we take up our role as the online safety regulator.
Document
Date published
User-generated content-enabled frauds and scams: short report (PDF, 274.8 KB)
17 May 2023
Mitigating illegal harms: a snapshot (PDF, 429.0 KB)
17 May 2023
Experiences of using online services
The Online Experiences Tracker is a quantitative tracking survey that examines people's attitudes to, and experiences of using online services.
Tracking abuse on Twitter against football players in the 2021-22 Premier League season
To understand the problem of footballing abuse, we commissioned The Alan Turing Institute to analyse more than 2.3 million tweets directed at Premier League footballers during the 2021-22 season.
